data.description: You can cast a spell that requires concentration while you have other spells of 1st level or higher active (see below). You lose the ability to concentrate on a spell if you are incapacitated, if you take certain damage, or when its duration expires.

A force of asphodelus energy encloses a creature or object of Small size or smaller within a shimmering sphere. Within the duration, nothing—not physical effects—can pass through the barrier in either direction.
A character can concentrate on a spell for only a limited time before losing concentration.

target a dance place: begins comic in shuffling, its and Creatures for duration. feet, capering the to spell. can't charmed immune this to spell. are be Adancing to must ali movement creature use its dance leaving space has without its and disadvantage on and rolls. saving Dexterity throws attack While the is by spell, creatures target affected this other have it. As on rolls advantage attack against an action, dancing a makes Wisdom saving creature a throw regain to control of itself. On successful the ends.

This illusion takes root in the mind of a creature you see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, you create a phantasmal creature or visible object or phenomenon of your choice that is larger than a 10-foot cube and perceivable to the target for the duration of the spell. This has no effect on undead or constructs. The phantasm includes temperature and can include stimuli such as tapping that are evident only to the target. The target can use an action to examine the phantasm with an Intelligence check (Investigation) against a DC set by you. If the check succeeds, the target realizes that the phantasm is an illusion, and the spell ends. While affected by this spell, the target treats any outcome involving the phantasm as real. For example, if the target attempts to walk across a phantasmal bridge that spans a chasm and falls once it steps onto the bridge, the target survives the fall but believes the bridge exists and even rationalizes its fall with some explanation such as having slipped or being knocked off by a strong wind. An affected target is convinced of the reality of the illusion to the point where it can take damage from the phantasm created to appear as a creature attacking it, or a similar hazard that logically deals damage. Each turn, a phantasm can deal 1d6 psychic damage to the target and radiates shadows and silence such that any creature within 30 feet of the phantasm is concealed from detection by you and your companions.
